steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb. The phrase "Even back in the 1860s" anchors the sentence firmly in the past. The subject "nearly 30% of the city's population" is a singular noun phrase, so the verb must be past tense and singular.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is being': present progressive. Contradicts the past time marker "in the 1860s." ✗
  • Choice B — 'was': simple past singular. Matches the past time reference and agrees with the singular subject. ✓
  • Choice C — 'will be': future tense. Contradicts "in the 1860s." ✗
  • Choice D — 'is': simple present singular. Contradicts the past time reference. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
